description: Agent D — relational/hash kernels per ABI §9. hash_dt and hash_combine use property tests (exact hash values are implementation-defined per ABI). join_hash_inner and join_hash_left use semantic fixtures: inputs are left/right 64-bit hash arrays (decimal strings) plus optional validity bit arrays; expected.pairs lists [l_idx, r_idx] in probe (left-row) order with duplicate right matches in build order. The runner owns ht_ptr/ht_cap/out_cap sizing and the -1-grow-and-retry protocol (see README §join). Null-validity rows (vp=0) never match even when both hash to H_NULL=0x9e3779b97f4a7c15. join_hash_left emits [l_idx,-1] for unmatched AND null-key left rows. unify_dict is DROPPED from v1 ABI; JS-side dictionary unification (src/memory/dictionary.ts) is the v1 path.

cases.name: hash_i32__equal_values_equal_hashes
cases.note: PROPERTY TEST: equal inputs must produce equal i64 hashes. The runner checks that positions in each group of 'equal_groups' have identical hash output values. It also checks positions in different groups have different hashes (collisions in test data would be a bug in the fixture, not the kernel).

cases.name: hash_i32__null_values_equal_hash
cases.note: PROPERTY TEST: null positions (vp=0) must all produce the same i64 hash as each other (a dedicated null-hash value). The null hash need not equal any particular value.

cases.name: hash_i32__empty
cases.note: len=0 is a valid no-op; no hashes written

cases.name: hash_i32__no_null_fast_path
cases.note: Omitting vp passes validity_ptr=0 (all-valid fast path)

cases.name: hash_combine__same_inputs_same_output
cases.note: PROPERTY TEST: hash_combine(acc, add, len) modifies acc in-place. Two calls with identical (acc, add) arrays must produce identical results. The runner calls hash_combine twice with the same inputs and verifies the outputs match.

expected.note: output acc_hash values are implementation-defined; tested via determinism

cases.name: hash_combine__different_combinations_differ
cases.note: PROPERTY TEST: hash_combine([h1,h1], [h2,h3]) → output[0] should differ from output[1] when h2 != h3 (with high probability; collisions in test data would be a fixture bug).

cases.name: group_build__all_distinct
cases.note: All distinct hash values → each element gets its own group; group_count = len. i64 hash values as decimal strings.

cases.name: group_build__all_same
cases.note: All same hash → one group; group_count = 1

cases.name: group_build__mixed
cases.note: Three distinct hash values; partition structure must match input hash equality

cases.name: group_build__null_key_is_own_group
cases.note: Per dtypes.md §4.5: null key values form their own group in groupby (unlike equijoin where null!=null). The null-hash from hash_dt is a specific i64 that all nulls share → group_build assigns them to one group. The dedicated null hash value is chosen by the implementation; the fixture supplies it directly so we can test group_build in isolation.

inputs.note: Hash value '0' represents the null hash (implementation uses 0 or a dedicated value; here we test that three identical 'null' hashes form one group)

cases.name: join_hash_inner__basic
cases.note: Build on right side, probe left rows in order. left=[100,200,300], right=[200,100,200]. l[0]=100 matches r[1]; l[1]=200 matches r[0] then r[2] (build order); l[2]=300 no match → no output row for inner join.

cases.name: join_hash_inner__no_match
cases.note: No hash value in left appears in right → empty result.

cases.name: join_hash_inner__null_never_matches
cases.note: Null-validity rows (vp=0) never match even when hash values are identical (dtypes.md §4.5: 'null != null for equijoin'). l[0]=100 valid matches r[0]=100 valid. l[1] has vp=0 → no match output. r[1] has vp=0 → cannot be matched.

cases.name: join_hash_inner__duplicate_right_matches
cases.note: One left row matches multiple right rows; duplicates emitted in build (right) order.

cases.name: join_hash_left__basic
cases.note: Left join: unmatched left rows emit [l_idx, -1]. l[0]=100 no match → [0,-1]; l[1]=200 matches r[0] → [1,0]; l[2]=300 no match → [2,-1].

cases.name: join_hash_left__null_key_left
cases.note: Null-key left rows (vp=0) emit [l_idx,-1] per wasm-abi.md §9 ('join_hash_left emits (l_idx,-1) for unmatched/null-key left rows'). l[0]=100 valid matches r[0] → [0,0]; l[1] null → [1,-1]; l[2]=200 no match → [2,-1].

cases.name: join_hash_left__all_matched
cases.note: All left rows match; no -1 entries emitted.
